description: Wave 4 — multi-provider model diversity, then statistical credibility, then liveness. Wave 3 closed the real-LLM verification gap (US-071 ran a complete mini7_v1 game to a MAFIA win on Cerebras + DeepInfra, $0.66 across 70 calls, 100% parse-rate; US-073 ran a 3-clone gauntlet, all MAFIA, $3.08 across 210 calls). The remaining single-provider operational risk surfaced during US-071: Cerebras `zai-glm-4.7` exhibited a ~44% transient failure rate during an earlier full-game run, masked only by DeepInfra's different-model fallback. Wave 4 closes this in three arcs. (1) Operational reliability + roster diversity (US-079..US-082): same-model multi-host fallback (Cerebras GLM-4.7 -> Z.AI GLM-4.7 hosts the same weights at a different endpoint), plus three new model identities — Z.AI GLM-5.1, DeepInfra Gemma-4-26B-A4B-it, Xiaomi Mimo-v2.5 / Mimo-v2.5-pro. Each new provider gets a real cassette recording (per US-072 discipline), a quirks probe, and a `live_llm` contract test entry. (2) Statistical credibility (US-083..US-084): one heterogeneous 7-seat game (each seat a different model) proves the engine survives the lineup, then a 10-game tournament with shuffled seat permutations tightens the per-faction Wilson CIs to a width <0.40 — the moment the leaderboard becomes citable. (3) Liveness (US-085): the wave-2 async scheduler gets its first real job — a cron-driven recurring heterogeneous gauntlet that keeps the public leaderboard fresh without manual `padrino gauntlet run` invocations. Quality gates from Waves 1-3 carry forward unchanged (ruff, ruff format, mypy strict, pytest, 85% core-engine coverage, AST purity firewall, no-wallclock firewall, `live_llm` cassette job, parse-rate quality gate on every real-LLM integration test). The `integration` marker continues to gate any test that needs a real provider key. Hard rules from Waves 1-3 are inherited verbatim: nothing under `src/padrino/core/` may import db / llm / api / runner or call wall-clock / network / random; mafia chat is parsed only via the `action` field; ranked observations never leak role, faction, model identity, ratings, clone index, or other games' transcripts.

userStories.title: Same-model multi-host fallback + Z.AI GLM-4.7 as Cerebras fallback
userStories.description: As an operator running real-LLM gauntlets, I need `RoutingPolicy` to support N alternate hosts for the SAME model identity so a Cerebras GLM-4.7 outage / rate-limit / transient 5xx routes to Z.AI's GLM-4.7 endpoint (same weights, different host) before falling through to the existing different-model fallback. This preserves the leaderboard's per-model identity (rating updates still credit GLM-4.7 regardless of which host served the request) and gives the operational reliability that single-host routing cannot.
acceptanceCriteria: Extend `padrino.llm.adapter.RoutingPolicy` with an optional `same_model_hosts: tuple[SameModelHost, ...] = ()` field where `SameModelHost(provider: str, litellm_model_id: str, auth_secret_ref: str, api_base: str | None = None)`. The existing `primary_model` / `fallback_model` (different-model fallback) fields are unchanged

`LiteLlmAdapter._call_model` (and the retry+fallback orchestration above it) tries `primary_model` first, then iterates `same_model_hosts` in order, then falls back to `fallback_model` last. Each host gets its own retry policy attempt budget; same-host retries do NOT consume cross-host attempts

New `AdapterStatus` literal `same_model_fallback_ok` distinguishes a successful same-model multi-host fallback from a different-model `fallback_ok`. Both count as success in `_PARSED_OK_STATUSES` for the integration parse-rate gate

Settings gains `padrino_cerebras_zai_glm47_zai_fallback: bool = True` (yes the name is long, deliberately — read like a sentence). When True and `ZAI_API_KEY` resolves, `Settings.build_routing_policy()` injects a `SameModelHost(provider='zai', litellm_model_id='openai/glm-4.7', api_base='https://api.z.ai/api/paas/v4/', auth_secret_ref='env:ZAI_API_KEY')` for any AgentBuild whose `primary_model='cerebras/zai-glm-4.7'`. When False or the env is missing, the same-model host list is empty and behavior is identical to today
New module `padrino.llm.secrets` already supports `env:VAR`; verify `resolve_secret('env:ZAI_API_KEY')` works end-to-end with the existing US-050 seam (no new code path needed there)

New unit test `tests/llm/test_litellm_adapter.py::test_same_model_fallback_routes_to_alternate_host` (a) stubs `litellm.acompletion` so the first call raises `litellm.exceptions.RateLimitError`, the second call (same-model host) returns valid JSON, and asserts `result.status == 'same_model_fallback_ok'` and that the different-model fallback was NOT exercised. (b) A second test confirms same-model exhaustion still triggers the different-model fallback

New integration test `tests/integration/test_same_model_fallback.py::test_cerebras_429_routes_to_zai_glm47` (marked `@pytest.mark.integration`, skipped on missing `ZAI_API_KEY` AND `CEREBRAS_API_KEY`) issues a real call against Cerebras GLM-4.7 with the same-model Z.AI host configured; asserts that on a successful run, BOTH cassettes are exercise-able (i.e. the same-model path works at all). To deliberately trigger the fallback in CI, monkeypatch the primary's litellm call to raise once

`padrino.api.routes.public.models_leaderboard` (US-067) keys per-model rows by the AgentBuild's `model_id` literal — confirm that a same-model multi-host fallback does NOT bifurcate the leaderboard row. Add an explicit assertion in `tests/api/test_models_leaderboard.py`
Document the same-model fallback path in `docs/deployment/byo-model.md` with a worked example for Cerebras → Z.AI GLM-4.7 routing
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run ruff format --check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes
userStories.notes: Landed 2026-05-22. RoutingPolicy now carries `same_model_hosts: tuple[SameModelHost, ...]`; LiteLlmAdapter iterates primary → same-model hosts → different-model fallback with per-host retry budgets and per-host api_base/api_key forwarding. New status `same_model_fallback_ok` distinguishes the cross-host success path. Settings gained `padrino_zai_api_base` (defaulting to the Z.AI Coding Plan endpoint `https://api.z.ai/api/coding/paas/v4` — the General API is also supported via env override) plus `padrino_cerebras_zai_glm47_zai_fallback` and `Settings.build_routing_policy()` that injects the Z.AI same-model host when the env + flag conditions are met. Verified end-to-end against real providers: forced-fallback integration test passed (Cerebras 429 → Z.AI GLM-4.7 served the call) after switching to the Coding Plan base URL. Leaderboard non-bifurcation invariant explicitly asserted in `tests/api/test_public_models.py`.

userStories.title: Z.AI GLM-5.1 as a distinct model candidate
userStories.description: As a benchmark publisher, I need GLM-5.1 (Z.AI's new flagship, OpenAI-compatible at api.z.ai/api/paas/v4/) to be a registerable provider+model so it can stand on the leaderboard alongside DeepSeek-V4-Flash and Cerebras GLM-4.7 with its own ratings and faction-stratified statistics.
acceptanceCriteria: New `ModelProvider` row in the bootstrap seed (`padrino.bootstrap`): provider_name='zai', api_base='https://api.z.ai/api/paas/v4/', auth_secret_ref='env:ZAI_API_KEY'. Migration is a data-only seed; no schema change needed if US-082's `api_base` column lands first; otherwise add a forward-compatible `ModelProvider.api_base` column in a new migration

New `ModelConfig` row: provider_id=zai, model_name='glm-5.1', litellm_model_id='openai/glm-5.1' (LiteLLM treats Z.AI as OpenAI-compatible when given `api_base`), inference_params from existing defaults

Quirks probe at `scripts/probe_zai_glm51.py` (gitignored output; the probe is the script): issues one canonical mini7 observation against the real endpoint and prints raw_response. Run it once, document any fence / preamble / trailing-text quirks discovered, and extend `padrino.llm.litellm_adapter._strip_code_fence` (or add a sibling normalizer) plus a `tests/llm/test_litellm_adapter.py` unit test if needed. If GLM-5.1 emits clean unfenced JSON, document that in the story notes and skip the normalizer change

New cassette `tests/llm/cassettes/zai/{canonical_response,malformed_response}.yaml` recorded against the real endpoint with secrets scrubbed (`authorization`, `api_key`, any organization header). The canonical cassette MUST contain a real recorded response, not a hand-authored synthetic one — leverage the US-072 record flow with `PADRINO_RECORD_LLM=1 ZAI_API_KEY=...`

Extend `tests/llm/test_litellm_contract.py` parametrize set with a `zai` entry (synthetic_canonical=False since we have a key). The `live_llm`-marked canonical_response test must pass against the recorded cassette
`tests/llm/cassettes/zai/*.yaml` passes the existing secret-scrub audit (`test_cassettes_have_no_secret_shaped_substrings`)

`scripts/audit_git_history_for_secrets.sh` extended to include the Z.AI key prefix pattern (`50cb...`-style 32-char hex + dot + 16-char alphanum; verify the exact prefix shape from the real key before pattern-matching). Update the audit's allowlist if any cassette location triggers a false positive
Update `docs/deployment/byo-model.md` Z.AI section with the exact `PADRINO_RECORD_LLM=1` re-record command and the grep verification line

userStories.notes: Landed 2026-05-24. Z.AI GLM-5.1 is registerable through the existing providers.yaml bootstrap path as provider `zai` with `ModelProvider.base_url=https://api.z.ai/api/coding/paas/v4`, `auth_secret_ref=env:ZAI_API_KEY`, and `ModelConfig.litellm_model_id=openai/glm-5.1`. Added `scripts/probe_zai_glm51.py`, recorded real Z.AI canonical + malformed VCR cassettes, extended the live_llm contract grid, and added Z.AI hex-dot key detection to cassette and history secret audits. Manual probe emitted unfenced JSON; the recorded canonical cassette used a normal markdown `json` code fence already handled by `_strip_code_fence`, so no adapter normalizer change was needed. Verified with ruff, ruff format, mypy, pytest -m 'not integration', and pytest -m live_llm --live-llm.

userStories.title: Xiaomi token-plan provider with custom base_url + Mimo-v2.5 + Mimo-v2.5-pro
userStories.description: As a benchmark publisher, I need to integrate Xiaomi's token-plan endpoint (OpenAI-compatible at a custom base URL: https://token-plan-sgp.xiaomimimo.com/v1) so Mimo-v2.5 and Mimo-v2.5-pro can join the leaderboard. This story is the only Wave-4 entry that requires architectural plumbing — the adapter must pass per-provider `api_base` through to LiteLLM rather than relying on the model_id prefix.
acceptanceCriteria: Extend `padrino.db.models.ModelProvider` with a nullable `api_base: str | None` column. New migration adds the column with a defaultable backfill (NULL for existing providers; LiteLLM defaults handle them). Update the bootstrap seed to set `api_base='https://token-plan-sgp.xiaomimimo.com/v1'` for the new Xiaomi row
Extend `Settings` with `xiaomi_api_key: str | None = None` and `xiaomi_base_url: str = 'https://token-plan-sgp.xiaomimimo.com/v1'`

Extend `padrino.llm.litellm_adapter.LiteLlmAdapter` to accept an optional `api_base: str | None` and forward it to `litellm.acompletion` via the documented `api_base=...` kwarg. The same-model multi-host fallback (US-079) already routes `api_base` per host; this story extends the seam to the primary call site too

Two new `ModelConfig` rows under the new Xiaomi provider: model_name='mimo-v2.5' and 'mimo-v2.5-pro', both `litellm_model_id='openai/<name>'` (LiteLLM uses the OpenAI translator when given `api_base`)

Quirks probe at `scripts/probe_xiaomi_mimo.py` (parametrized by model_name): one canonical mini7 observation per model, raw_response printed. Document any quirks. Mimo is a Chinese-trained model — watch for occasional Chinese-language preambles or `</think>` tags

New cassettes `tests/llm/cassettes/xiaomi/{mimo_v25_canonical_response,mimo_v25_malformed_response,mimo_v25_pro_canonical_response,mimo_v25_pro_malformed_response}.yaml`, recorded against the real endpoint with secrets scrubbed. Xiaomi keys start with `tp-` — add that prefix to the scrub patterns

Extend `scripts/audit_git_history_for_secrets.sh` patterns with the `tp-` Xiaomi prefix; verify the audit returns 0 against current head after the cassettes land

userStories.title: Heterogeneous real-LLM gauntlet: one distinct model per seat
userStories.description: As a benchmark publisher, I need at least one real-LLM gauntlet that assigns a DIFFERENT model identity to every seat — not a single model played by 7 clones — so the leaderboard's faction-balance and per-model rating deltas reflect head-to-head competition between models, not self-play variance. This is the story that turns Padrino from a stress test into a benchmark.
acceptanceCriteria: Extend `padrino.gauntlets` (the gauntlet runner that lives outside pure-core) to accept a per-seat `agent_build_assignments: Mapping[PublicPlayerId, AgentBuild] | None = None` argument. When provided, each seat gets the specified AgentBuild instead of the gauntlet-default. The existing `clone_count` path remains the default and works as before

Roster for the new heterogeneous integration test: P01=Cerebras GLM-4.7 (with Z.AI same-model fallback from US-079), P02=Z.AI GLM-5.1, P03=DeepInfra DeepSeek-V4-Flash, P04=DeepInfra Gemma-4-26B-A4B-it, P05=Xiaomi Mimo-v2.5, P06=Xiaomi Mimo-v2.5-pro, P07=(repeat the model with the lowest cost per call to stay under cap; document the choice)

New integration test `tests/integration/test_heterogeneous_gauntlet.py::test_heterogeneous_gauntlet_runs` (marked `@pytest.mark.integration`, skipped when ANY of {CEREBRAS_API_KEY, ZAI_API_KEY, DEEPINFRA_API_KEY, XIAOMI_API_KEY} is missing) runs ONE game (not three — cost-managed) with the roster above; asserts: (a) terminal_result is TOWN, MAFIA, or DRAW (no winner-not-DRAW because heterogeneous gameplay variance is real), (b) parse-rate >= 70% (the now-fixed `ok + fallback_ok` count), (c) every model_id produced at least one ok / fallback_ok / same_model_fallback_ok call (the per-model coverage check)
Cost cap $4.00 for the single game (covers ~70 calls * up to ~$0.05 worst-case for the larger models like Mimo-v2.5-pro). Test prints actual cost on success

`padrino.gauntlets.evaluation.evaluate_gauntlet` (US-077) called on the resulting gauntlet produces a `GauntletReport` with per-model `rating_deltas` populated; assert each model in the roster appears in `rating_deltas` with non-default mu/sigma

Dashboard update: the `/gauntlets/[id]/report` route already exists from US-077; verify it renders the heterogeneous roster without UI regression (the Playwright spec uses page.route fixtures so this is verified at unit level; a manual smoke against the real gauntlet in dev is acceptable)
Document the heterogeneous-roster API in `docs/deployment/byo-model.md` with a worked example
uv run ruff check . passes
uv run ruff format --check . passes
uv run mypy src tests passes
uv run pytest -m "not integration" passes
uv run coverage report --include='src/padrino/core/*' --fail-under=85 passes

userStories.title: Multi-game heterogeneous tournament with Wilson-CI statistical gate
userStories.description: As a benchmark publisher, I need at least 10 games of the heterogeneous roster from US-083 with shuffled seat permutations so the per-faction Wilson confidence intervals are tight enough to support public claims. A single game (US-083) is enough to prove the engine survives a 7-distinct-model lineup, but a one-game sample on a 2-vs-5 game produces a (0.0, 0.66) Wilson CI for either faction — not publishable. This story adds the N-game runner and the statistical gate that turns the engine into a benchmark people can cite.
acceptanceCriteria: Extend `padrino.gauntlets` runner to accept a `n_games: int = 1` parameter alongside the existing `agent_build_assignments: Mapping[PublicPlayerId, AgentBuild]` seam from US-083. When `n_games > 1`, the runner fires that many games sequentially against the SAME roster, but each game's seat assignment is a deterministic random permutation derived from `SeededRng(f'{gauntlet_seed}:{game_index}')` so the same roster plays multiple seat orderings. The existing `n_games=1` path stays identical to today's behaviour

Permutation invariant (asserted in a unit test): across N games, each AgentBuild appears in `floor(N * 7 / len(roster))` to `ceil(N * 7 / len(roster))` seats, so seat exposure is balanced. For a 7-model roster at N=10 each model plays exactly 10 seats (alternating role positions as the permutation rotates)

Extend `padrino.gauntlets.evaluation.evaluate_gauntlet` to compute and surface per-model `faction_seat_counts` (town_seats / mafia_seats observed per model) and per-(model, faction) `WilsonCIBand` in `GauntletReport`. The existing `faction_win_rates: list[FactionWinRate]` is now augmented with per-model breakdowns

New integration test `tests/integration/test_heterogeneous_gauntlet.py::test_heterogeneous_10game_tournament` (marked `@pytest.mark.integration`, skipped when ANY of CEREBRAS_API_KEY, ZAI_API_KEY, DEEPINFRA_API_KEY, XIAOMI_API_KEY is missing). Runs 10 games. Cost cap $20.00 — the test prints actual cost on success

Assertions: (a) all 10 games reach a terminal state (no engine crashes / hung games); (b) parse-rate >= 70% aggregated across all games (using the now-fixed `ok + fallback_ok + same_model_fallback_ok` count); (c) every model appears in at least 5 seats across the 10 games; (d) Wilson CI width for either faction's overall win-rate is < 0.40 (i.e. either faction's 95% CI is at most 40 percentage points wide — looser than a published benchmark but tight enough to be useful); (e) per-model rating deltas computed and the top-3 / bottom-3 models reported in test stdout for human eyeballing

CLI: `padrino gauntlet run --n-games N --roster path/to/roster.yaml --cost-cap-usd 20.0` maps to the new runner parameter. The roster YAML schema mirrors US-083's `agent_build_assignments` shape (one agent_build_id per public_player_id slot)
Document the multi-game protocol + the roster YAML shape in `docs/deployment/byo-model.md` with a worked example

userStories.title: Scheduled recurring heterogeneous gauntlet via the in-process scheduler
userStories.description: As a benchmark publisher, I need the heterogeneous N-game gauntlet (US-084) to fire on a cron-style schedule via Padrino's existing async scheduler (wave-2 US-054) so the public leaderboard reflects fresh head-to-head data without manual `padrino gauntlet run` invocations. Wave-2 shipped the scheduler scaffolding (heartbeats only); this story is its first real job and the moment Padrino becomes a live benchmark rather than a one-shot evaluation tool.
acceptanceCriteria: New `scheduled_gauntlets` table + migration `0014_scheduled_gauntlets.py` with columns: `id: uuid pk`, `name: text not null unique`, `schedule_cron: text not null` (5-field cron spec), `roster_spec_json: jsonb / text` (the US-084 roster YAML shape serialized), `n_games: int not null default 1`, `cost_cap_usd: numeric not null`, `enabled: bool not null default true`, `last_run_at: timestamptz nullable`, `last_run_gauntlet_id: uuid nullable fk -> gauntlets.id`, `next_run_at: timestamptz nullable`, `created_at: timestamptz not null default now()`

New `padrino.scheduler.gauntlet_job` module: registered as a scheduler job by `padrino.scheduler.bootstrap`. On each scheduler tick the job (a) queries `scheduled_gauntlets WHERE enabled=true AND (next_run_at IS NULL OR next_run_at <= now())`, (b) for each due row, materializes the roster_spec into AgentBuild assignments, fires `run_gauntlet(n_games=row.n_games, cost_cap_usd=row.cost_cap_usd)`, (c) updates `last_run_at`, `last_run_gauntlet_id`, and `next_run_at` (computed from `schedule_cron`). Cost overrun aborts the run mid-way and the partial Gauntlet row is left with `status='cost_capped'`

Cron parsing lives in a pure-core helper `padrino.core.scheduling.next_run_at(cron_expr: str, *, after: datetime) -> datetime` that takes 5-field cron syntax (`*/15 * * * *`, `0 2 * * *`, etc.) and returns the next firing time strictly after the supplied moment. Cron evaluation must be deterministic and free of wallclock reads (pure-core firewall — `after` is injected). Use the existing `croniter` library if it's already a dep; otherwise hand-roll a minimal field-by-field iterator. Either way: the helper lives under `src/padrino/core/scheduling/` and has at least 5 unit tests covering edge cases (DST, leap day, month boundary, every-Nth-minute, every-Nth-hour)

Admin routes: `POST /admin/scheduled-gauntlets` accepts `{name, schedule_cron, roster_spec, n_games, cost_cap_usd, enabled}`, validates that every `agent_build_id` referenced in `roster_spec` exists, persists the row, and returns the new id + the computed `next_run_at`. `PATCH /admin/scheduled-gauntlets/{id}` updates `enabled` / `schedule_cron` / `cost_cap_usd` (other fields are immutable — to change roster, create a new row). `DELETE /admin/scheduled-gauntlets/{id}` sets `enabled=false` and clears `next_run_at` so no future runs fire (the row stays for audit)

Public route: `GET /public/scheduled-gauntlets` returns `{schedules: [{name, schedule_cron_human, last_run_at, next_run_at, last_gauntlet_id, status}]}`. `schedule_cron_human` is a humanized form like 'every day at 02:00 UTC' computed via a small helper; the raw cron expr and `cost_cap_usd` are NOT in the public payload

Integration test `tests/integration/test_scheduled_gauntlet.py::test_one_shot_scheduled_gauntlet` (marked `@pytest.mark.integration`, gated on the same key set as US-084): creates a one-shot scheduled gauntlet with cron `* * * * *` and `n_games=1` and a low cost cap, advances the scheduler's injected clock by 60 s, asserts the gauntlet ran, `last_run_at` updated, and `last_run_gauntlet_id` points at a non-null Gauntlet with at least 1 completed game
Unit tests for the cron helper, the scheduled-gauntlet repository (CRUD + uniqueness on `name`), the public route's scrubbed shape, and the job's cost-cap-abort path
Documentation: new `docs/deployment/scheduled-gauntlets.md` covering the schedule shape, the YAML roster spec, how to disable a schedule, and the public leaderboard's update cadence under the new job
